data.title: Avenue Z Shopify Platinum Tie Highlights AI Search And Brand Ambitions
data.description:
• Avenue Z has been named a Shopify Platinum Partner, joining a small group of U.S. agencies with this status.
  • The agency is positioning around AI Search Optimization and Agentic Commerce within Shopify’s ecosystem.
  • Avenue Z also holds Platinum Partner status with TikTok, creating a dual relationship across commerce and content.
  • The platinum tier includes access to advanced Shopify features, beta programs, and tailored partner support.

For investors watching Shopify, ticker NasdaqGS:SHOP, this partnership adds another data point on how the company is leaning into AI driven commerce and larger brand relationships. The share price is currently $131.15, with a 1 year return of 56.8% and a 3 year return that is a very large multiple relative to the base period. Over 5 years, the return stands at 19.3%, reflecting a more moderate long term picture.

Recent shorter term returns show a mixed picture, with the stock up 18.4% over the past week and 6.0% over the past month, but down 16.6% year to date. As Shopify expands its partner ecosystem into AI search, agentic commerce, and deeper content integrations, investors may want to watch how these capabilities resonate with large brands that are considering or already using Shopify for enterprise scale commerce.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Shopify by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Shopify.

1 thing going right for Shopify that this headline doesn't cover.

Avenue Z joining Shopify’s Platinum Partner tier puts more AI focused execution capacity directly into the hands of mid sized and enterprise brands using Shopify. With design, development, performance media, AI Search Optimization and agentic commerce under one roof, Avenue Z can act as a full funnel operator for merchants that want to test how AI agents search, evaluate and transact on their behalf. For Shopify, that creates another channel to drive adoption of newer features such as AI centric search, storefront tooling and commerce workflows that are designed for machine driven rather than only human driven discovery. The dual Platinum relationship with TikTok is also relevant, because it tightens the link between short form content, social discovery and checkouts running on Shopify. For investors, the key takeaway is not the headline status itself, but that Shopify continues to attract agencies able to stitch together content, media and AI powered commerce into one offering. This may help the platform stay in the consideration set for brands comparing it with large competitors like Amazon, Adobe Commerce and Salesforce Commerce Cloud.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Shopify Narrative

  • This partnership aligns closely with the narrative that Shopify is leaning into AI tools and emerging channels, because Avenue Z is explicitly focused on AI Search Optimization and agentic commerce across the customer journey.
  • The news also tests a key narrative assumption that Shopify can keep scaling with larger brands, since execution now partly depends on agency partners like Avenue Z delivering results for complex clients.
  • The agency’s early focus on how AI agents discover and transact for consumers may not be fully reflected in existing narratives that concentrate more on merchant tools, payments and traditional social commerce integrations.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Shopify to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have highlighted that Shopify remains exposed to competition from large commerce platforms such as Amazon, Adobe and Salesforce, and partnerships like this do not remove pricing or margin pressures.
  • ⚠️ Execution risk sits with Avenue Z delivering real merchant outcomes from AI Search Optimization and agentic commerce, and if results disappoint, large brands may reassess their commitment to Shopify centric builds.
  • 🎁 Avenue Z’s Platinum status, plus Platinum status with TikTok, gives Shopify another distribution and implementation path for AI powered features, which can help merchants test new growth channels more quickly.
  • 🎁 The focus on full funnel services from storefront build to lifecycle marketing supports the view that Shopify can be a core operating system for brands rather than just a checkout plug in.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors should watch how many enterprise and upper mid market brands Avenue Z onboards to Shopify, and whether those merchants publicly reference AI driven search or agent led shopping results over time. Announcements like Reitmans’ multi brand migration give some early evidence of interest in Shopify at scale, but the question is how often large brands choose Shopify, rather than alternatives, when they want AI centric commerce builds. It is also worth tracking how often TikTok centric campaigns push traffic into Shopify stores through Avenue Z, because that link between content and checkouts is central to the promise of this partnership. Any future commentary from Shopify on the uptake of agentic commerce features, or on the performance of Platinum agencies as a group, would give useful signals on whether these partnerships are becoming a material growth driver or remain more of a supporting factor.

data.title: Do These 3 Checks Before Buying Athens International Airport S.A. (ATH:AIA) For Its Upcoming Dividend
data.description:
Athens International Airport S.A. (ATH:AIA) stock is about to trade ex-dividend in three days. The ex-dividend date is commonly two business days before the record date, which is the cut-off date for shareholders to be present on the company's books to be eligible for a dividend payment. The ex-dividend date is important as the process of settlement involves at least two full business days. So if you miss that date, you would not show up on the company's books on the record date. Meaning, you will need to purchase Athens International Airport's shares before the 22nd of April to receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 15th of May.

The company's next dividend payment will be €0.66 per share. Last year, in total, the company distributed €0.66 to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Athens International Airport has a trailing yield of 6.0% on the current stock price of €11.01. Dividends are an important source of income to many shareholders, but the health of the business is crucial to maintaining those dividends. So we need to check whether the dividend payments are covered, and if earnings are growing.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Dividends are usually paid out of company profits, so if a company pays out more than it earned then its dividend is usually at greater risk of being cut. Last year Athens International Airport paid out 97% of its profits as dividends to shareholders, suggesting the dividend is not well covered by earnings. Yet cash flow is typically more important than profit for assessing dividend sustainability, so we should always check if the company generated enough cash to afford its dividend. The company paid out 99% of its free cash flow over the last year, which we think is outside the ideal range for most businesses. Cash flows are usually much more volatile than earnings, so this could be a temporary effect - but we'd generally want to look more closely here.

Cash is slightly more important than profit from a dividend perspective, but given Athens International Airport's payouts were not well covered by either earnings or cash flow, we would be concerned about the sustainability of this dividend.

View our latest analysis for Athens International Airport

Click here to see the company's payout ratio, plus analyst estimates of its future dividends.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Companies with consistently growing earnings per share generally make the best dividend stocks, as they usually find it easier to grow dividends per share. If earnings decline and the company is forced to cut its dividend, investors could watch the value of their investment go up in smoke. It's encouraging to see Athens International Airport has grown its earnings rapidly, up 22% a year for the past five years. Athens International Airport's dividend was not well covered by earnings, although at least its earnings per share are growing quickly. Generally, when a company is growing this quickly and paying out all of its earnings as dividends, it can suggest either that the company is borrowing heavily to fund its growth, or that earnings growth is likely to slow due to lack of reinvestment.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. In the last two years, Athens International Airport has lifted its dividend by approximately 41% a year on average. It's exciting to see that both earnings and dividends per share have grown rapidly over the past few years.

Final Takeaway

Is Athens International Airport an attractive dividend stock, or better left on the shelf? While it's nice to see earnings per share growing, we're curious about how Athens International Airport intends to continue growing, or maintain the dividend in a downturn given that it's paying out such a high percentage of its earnings and cashflow. It's not the most attractive proposition from a dividend perspective, and we'd probably give this one a miss for now.

Having said that, if you're looking at this stock without much concern for the dividend, you should still be familiar of the risks involved with Athens International Airport. We've identified 2 warning signs with Athens International Airport (at least 1 which is a bit concerning), and understanding them should be part of your investment process.

Generally, we wouldn't recommend just buying the first dividend stock you see. Here's a curated list of interesting stocks that are strong dividend payers.

data.title: Is It Time To Reconsider Life360 (ASX:360) After The Uber Partnership News?
data.description:
• Wondering if Life360 at around A$21.35 offers fair value or if the share price is getting ahead of itself? This article walks through the key numbers so you can judge that for yourself.
  • The stock has returned 9.6% over the past week and 9.6% over the last month, while year to date it is down 34.2% and up 8.4% over the past year, with a very large 3 year return of 334.8% and 315.8% over 5 years.
  • Recent attention around Life360 has centred on its position in the software sector and how investors are weighing its growth profile against risk. That context helps explain why the share price has been active recently as the market reassesses what it is prepared to pay.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Life360 scores 4 out of 6. This sets up a closer look at how different valuation methods line up today and points to an even more useful way to think about value at the end of this article.

Life360 delivered 8.4% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Software industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Life360 Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those amounts back to what they might be worth today.

For Life360, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $82.4 million. Analyst forecasts extend to 2028, with Simply Wall St extrapolating beyond that. For example, projected free cash flow is $133.9 million in 2026, $184.2 million in 2027 and $267.8 million in 2028, with further estimates running out to 2035 in the hundreds of millions of dollars each year.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of A$43.69 per share. Compared with the recent share price around A$21.35, this output implies the stock is 51.1% undervalued on this method alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Life360 is undervalued by 51.1%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 11 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Life360.

Approach 2: Life360 Price vs Earnings

P/E is a common way to value profitable companies because it links what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that share. In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk tend to justify a higher P/E ratio, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower, more cautious multiple.

Life360 currently trades on a P/E of 24.6x. That is in line with the wider Software industry average of 24.6x, but sits well below the peer group average of 58.8x. On the surface, that suggests the market is assigning a more moderate rating than many peers.

Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 28.9x. This is an estimate of the P/E that might be reasonable given Life360’s earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk characteristics. Because it blends these company specific factors, the Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry, which may have very different growth, profitability and risk.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 28.9x with the current P/E of 24.6x suggests the shares are trading below that company specific reference point.

Result: UNDERVALUED

You can pick or build a story that matches how you see Life360, whether that is closer to a cautious Fair Value of A$25.00 or a more optimistic A$53.00.   • The stock has logged returns of 12.0% over the last 7 days, 29.9% over 30 days, 45.3% year to date and 65.5% over the past year, which has sharpened the focus on what is already built into the price.

Arm Holdings scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Arm Holdings Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today to reflect risk and the time value of money.

For Arm Holdings, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $1.16b. Analyst and extrapolated projections suggest Free Cash Flow could reach $6.73b by the 2031 financial year, with intermediate annual projections between 2026 and 2035 stepping up from roughly $1.23b to $12.15b before discounting.

When all those projected cash flows are discounted back to today, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $78.04 per share. Compared with the recent share price of roughly $166.73, this implies the stock is around 113.7% above the DCF estimate, which points to Arm Holdings trading at a rich premium to this cash flow based valuation.

Result: OVERVALUED

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Arm Holdings.

Approach 2: Arm Holdings Price vs Sales

For profitable companies, price based multiples are a quick way to see what the market is paying for each unit of earnings, sales or book value. For Arm Holdings, P/S is the preferred metric, which is often useful for comparing businesses where revenue is a key focus and earnings can be affected by accounting or investment cycles.

What counts as a “normal” P/S ratio usually reflects how quickly investors expect revenue to grow and how confident they are about the risks around that outlook. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk tend to support higher P/S multiples.

Arm Holdings currently trades on a P/S ratio of 37.91x, compared with the Semiconductor industry average of 6.53x and a peer average of 11.35x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Arm Holdings is 45.83x, which reflects factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks. This Fair Ratio aims to give a more tailored view than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for the company’s own characteristics rather than assuming all semiconductor stocks deserve similar multiples.

Since the current P/S of 37.91x is below the Fair Ratio of 45.83x, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 19 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Arm Holdings Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. This is where Narratives come in. Narratives give you a simple, story-driven framework on Simply Wall St’s Community page that links your view of Arm Holdings to specific assumptions for future revenue, earnings and margins. They connect those assumptions to a Fair Value, and compare that Fair Value to the current price to help you decide whether the stock looks expensive or attractive on your terms. They then keep that view up to date as new news or earnings arrive. This is why one Narrative on Arm might point to a Fair Value near the low end of recent estimates around US$39 per share, while another might sit closer to the high end near US$217, both grounded in different but transparent sets of expectations.

For Arm Holdings however we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading Arm Holdings Narratives:

🐂 Arm Holdings Bull Case

Fair value in this bullish narrative: US$205.00 per share

Implied discount to this fair value at US$166.73: about 18.7%

Assumed annual revenue growth rate: 27.38%

  • Frames Arm as a beneficiary of AI related CPU demand, with data center, edge AI and a large software ecosystem supporting higher royalties and margins across multiple end markets.
  • Uses analyst assumptions that revenue grows at 27.4% a year for three years and profit margins rise from 17.1% to 41.8%, with earnings modeled at US$4.0b by around March 2029.

🐻 Arm Holdings Bear Case

  • Highlights that Arm already serves a wide customer base, so future growth may rely more on industry expansion and product refreshes than on signing many new large customers.

Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move from SEK 1.3b in Q1 2025 to SEK 1.4b in Q4 2025, while quarterly EPS ranged from 1.02 SEK in Q2 to 3.87 SEK in Q4. TTM earnings of SEK 4.0b contrast with 5.2% revenue growth

  • Over the last twelve months, AB Sagax generated SEK 5.4b in revenue and SEK 4.0b in net income, while revenue is described as growing at roughly 5.2% a year and earnings over five years fell about 17.4% per year with a slight 0.08% annual decline forecast ahead.
  • What stands out for a bearish view is that modest revenue growth sits alongside weaker earnings trends, as shown by the trailing net profit margin of 75.3% being lower than last year and the 5 year annual earnings decline of about 17.4%. Critics highlight this as a sign that reported profitability is not translating into consistent earnings strength even though quarterly revenue stepped up from SEK 1,307m in Q1 2025 to SEK 1,390m in Q4 2025.
    • Bearish arguments that earnings quality is under pressure are supported by trailing EPS slipping from 11.31 SEK in Q4 2024 to 8.71 SEK in Q4 2025 despite the SEK 5.4b revenue figure.
    • At the same time, Q4 2025 net income of SEK 1,799m is well above Q2 2025 net income of SEK 476m, which shows that quarter to quarter swings are large and gives bears more data points to question how repeatable the strongest quarters might be.
On these numbers, skeptics will want a closer look at how consistent earnings really are over time and how that lines up with their own expectations before taking the bearish case further 🐻 AB Sagax Bear Case.

High 75.3% margin and cash flow to debt gap

  • The trailing net profit margin is put at 75.3% while debt is flagged as not being well covered by operating cash flow, which means that even with SEK 4.0b of net income on SEK 5.4b of revenue, cash generation relative to debt is a key watchpoint.
  • Supporters of a more positive read on the business model will notice that such a high reported margin and a 1.9% dividend yield sit alongside the revenue growth figure of about 5.18% a year. Yet the same data underline that operating cash flow does not sufficiently cover debt, so any bullish stance has to square strong reported profitability with this balance sheet risk.
    • Revenue rising from SEK 4.8b on a trailing basis in Q3 2024 to SEK 5.4b in Q4 2025 helps the bullish angle that the asset base is producing more top line, even if earnings per share have moved the other way.
    • However, the combination of a 75.3% margin and flagged debt coverage risk means bulls need to focus not just on income statement strength but also on how quickly cash flow can support obligations relative to that high accounting profitability.

P/E of 22.3x and price far above DCF fair value

  • With a share price of SEK 194.50, the stock trades on a P/E of 22.3x compared with a Swedish real estate industry average of 12.4x and a peer group average of 26.4x, while a DCF fair value of SEK 43.46 sits well below the current price.

data.title: Roku Reaches 100 Million Households And Tests Its Connected TV Clout
data.description:
• Roku, Inc. (NasdaqGS:ROKU) reports that its platform is now used by more than 100 million active streaming households globally.
  • This audience figure reflects Roku's current scale in the streaming platform market and its reach with viewers worldwide.
  • The company highlights this milestone as relevant for its relationships with advertisers and content partners.

Roku operates a connected TV platform that links viewers, advertisers, and content providers in one place, with revenue tied to both devices and its software platform. The 100 million household figure positions Roku among the larger global streaming platform players at a time when viewing continues to shift from traditional TV to internet based services. For investors, this reported scale helps frame discussions about user engagement, advertising demand, and content distribution on the platform.

As Roku expands further outside the US, the mix of domestic and international households could influence advertising strategies and content partnerships over time. For your watchlist, this milestone may be useful context when you assess how NasdaqGS:ROKU seeks to monetize viewing hours, attract global ad budgets, and differentiate its platform from other connected TV ecosystems.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Roku by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Roku.

We've flagged 0 risks for Roku. See which could impact your investment.

For Roku, crossing 100 million active streaming households is primarily a scale story, and scale is the core asset in connected TV. A larger footprint can make Roku more relevant to big brands that want broad reach, as well as to smaller advertisers that care about precise audience targeting. It also gives Roku more leverage when negotiating carriage terms with subscription services that want access to that user base. At the same time, size does not eliminate execution risk. Roku is still competing with platforms from Amazon, Google and Apple for time on the living-room screen, and the recent patent complaint at the US International Trade Commission highlights that legal and hardware related issues can sit in the background even as user metrics look strong.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Roku Narrative

  • The reported 100 million households align with the narrative that a growing active-user base can support higher usage of Roku Ads Manager and other ad tools as brands continue shifting budgets toward connected TV.
  • Stronger platform influence may also raise expectations for international expansion and proprietary content. This could increase pressure on Roku to keep spending efficiently while competition from Amazon Fire TV, Google TV and others stays intense.
  • The milestone does not fully capture hardware or regulatory risks, such as the ITC investigation into imports of streaming devices, which may not be fully reflected in longer term platform growth assumptions.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Roku to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ A complaint at the US International Trade Commission focused on streaming devices and smart TVs introduces the possibility of import restrictions or added costs that could affect Roku hardware availability.
  • ⚠️ Heavy reliance on advertising makes Roku sensitive to any pullback or reallocation of ad budgets toward rival platforms such as YouTube on Google TV or Amazon Fire TV, which could influence monetization of its large user base.
  • 🎁 Reaching over 100 million streaming households gives Roku a stronger position when negotiating with content partners that want better visibility for their apps and channels on connected TVs.
  • 🎁 A larger audience can support more data driven ad products and potential new revenue lines around subscriptions and performance marketing, which are already key themes in Roku’s broader growth narrative.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, pay attention to how Roku links the 100 million household figure to concrete metrics such as ad impressions sold, fill rates and engagement on The Roku Channel. Updates on international user mix will matter for understanding how much of the scale sits outside the US and what that means for ad pricing. Any progress reports on the ITC investigation or on hardware sourcing will also be important, because disruptions on the device side could affect future account growth even if current usage looks strong.
